title: Make Picture Graphs
essential_question: How can you make a picture graph to show data in a table?
strategies: Divide the data value by the key to find number of symbols

vertical_alignment.prerequisite: G2 — Organize data in up to 4 categories; use tally marks
vertical_alignment.current: G3 — Make scaled picture graphs (value ÷ key = symbols; half symbols)
vertical_alignment.successor: G4 — Multi-step problems using data in various representations

unit_intro_message: 이번 단원에서는 빈도표 데이터를 이용해 그림 그래프를 직접 만드는 방법을 배웁니다. 키를 선택하고, 각 값을 키로 나누어 심볼 수를 구하며, 반 심볼 사용법도 익힙니다.
unit_close_message: 잘 했어요! 이제 빈도표에서 그림 그래프를 만들 수 있습니다. 다음 레슨에서는 막대 그래프 읽기를 학습합니다.

pretest.question: A frequency table shows: Comedy 8, Drama 4, Sports 6. If each symbol = 2 students, how many symbols do you draw for Comedy?

expected_errors.description: 키(÷2) 무시하고 원래 값(8) 그대로 제출
expected_errors.citation: Friel et al. (2001) p.130 — 그림 그래프 스케일 오독

learn.title: Steps to Make a Picture Graph
learn.content: Step 1: Write the title. Step 2: Choose a key (decide how many each symbol represents). Step 3: Divide each data value by the key number to find how many symbols to draw. Step 4: Draw the symbols. Step 5: Write the key at the bottom.

learn.title: Choosing a Good Key
learn.content: Pick a key number that divides evenly into most or all of your data values. Common keys: 2, 3, 4, 5, 10. If a value doesn't divide evenly, you can use a half symbol.

learn.title: Half Symbols
learn.content: If each symbol = 4 and you need to show 6, draw 1 full symbol and 1 half symbol (4 + 2 = 6). Half a symbol = half the key value.

learn.title: Guided Example — Making a Picture Graph
learn.content: Frequency table: Cats 6, Dogs 10, Birds 4. Key = 2. Step 1: Cats → 6 ÷ 2 = 3 symbols. Step 2: Dogs → 10 ÷ 2 = 5 symbols. Step 3: Birds → 4 ÷ 2 = 2 symbols. Draw the symbols in each row and write the key at the bottom.
learn.math_note: Cats=6÷2=3, Dogs=10÷2=5, Birds=4÷2=2. 규칙: 심볼 수 = 값 ÷ 키.

learn.title: Half Symbols in Practice
learn.content: When a data value is NOT evenly divisible by the key, use a half symbol. Example: Key = 2. Red 9, Blue 6, Green 7. Red: 9 ÷ 2 = 4½ → draw 4 full + 1 half symbol. Blue: 6 ÷ 2 = 3 → draw 3 full symbols. Green: 7 ÷ 2 = 3½ → draw 3 full + 1 half symbol. Half symbol = half the key value (here, ½ symbol = 1 student).
learn.math_note: 키=2: Red=4½심볼, Blue=3심볼, Green=3½심볼. 반 심볼=키÷2=1.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 2 Lesson 2.3 — Make Picture Graphs
concept_source.description: 그림 그래프 만들기: 키 선택, 값÷키=심볼 수, 반 심볼 사용

learn.title: Choosing the Right Key
learn.content: Test your key by dividing each data value. The best key gives whole numbers or clean halves (÷ 0.5) for ALL categories. Example: Basketball 12, Soccer 18, Tennis 6, Baseball 9. Test key=3: 12÷3=4 ✓, 18÷3=6 ✓, 6÷3=2 ✓, 9÷3=3 ✓ → key=3 works! Test key=4: 12÷4=3 ✓, 18÷4=4.5 ✓, 6÷4=1.5 ✓, 9÷4=2.25 ✗ → key=4 doesn't work cleanly.
learn.math_note: key=3 선택 이유: 12,18,6,9 모두 3의 배수. key=4는 9÷4=2.25 (불가).

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 2 Lesson 2.3 — Make Picture Graphs

learn.title: Math Talk: Which Key Needs a Half Symbol?
learn.content: Frequency table: Band 20, Art 15, Drama 10. Lily uses key=5: Band=4, Art=3, Drama=2 — all WHOLE symbols. ✓ Her friend uses key=10: Band=2, Art=1½, Drama=1 — Art needs a HALF symbol. Both keys are valid, but key=5 gives a more precise graph with no half symbols. (Smaller key = more symbols = more precise. Larger key = fewer symbols = simpler graph.)
learn.math_talk_prompt: Band=20, Art=15, Drama=10 데이터에서 key=5: Band=4, Art=3, Drama=2 (반 심볼 없음) vs key=10: Band=2, Art=1½, Drama=1 (Art에 반 심볼 필요). 왜 key=5가 key=10보다 정밀한가요? 키를 작게 할수록/크게 할수록 그래프가 어떻게 달라지나요? 어떤 상황에서 큰 키(key=10)를 쓰는 것이 나을까요?
learn.math_note: key=5: 모두 정수 ✓. key=10: Art=1½ 반 심볼 필요. 작은 키=더 정밀, 큰 키=더 단순.

learn.title: Lesson Summary: Make Picture Graphs
learn.content: In this lesson you learned how to: ① Choose a key: pick a number that divides evenly into most data values. ② Find symbols: data value ÷ key = number of symbols to draw. ③ Use half symbols: if remainder = ½ of key, draw a half symbol. ④ Count total symbols: divide each value by key, then ADD the symbol counts. ⑤ Write the key at the bottom of every picture graph.
learn.lesson_summary: 그림 그래프 만들기 마스터: ①키 선택: 모든 데이터 값이 깔끔하게 나눠지는 수 ②심볼 수=값÷키 ③반 심볼: 나머지=키÷2일 때 사용 ④총 심볼=각 카테고리 심볼 수 합산 ⑤그래프 하단에 키 표기 필수
learn.math_note: 핵심: 값÷키=심볼 수. 나머지가 키÷2이면 반 심볼 사용.

try.question: Frequency table: Music 15, Art 10, Dance 20, Drama 5. Which key lets ALL categories use whole number symbols only (no half symbols)?
try.explanation: key=5: Music=15÷5=3 ✓, Art=10÷5=2 ✓, Dance=20÷5=4 ✓, Drama=5÷5=1 ✓. key=2: Dance=20÷2=10 ✓ but others give large counts. key=3: Dance=20÷3≈6.67 ✗. key=4: Art=10÷4=2.5 ✗. Only key=5 gives all whole numbers.
solution_steps: key=2: Drama=5÷2=2½ ✗. key=3: Dance=20÷3=6⅔ ✗. key=4: Art=10÷4=2½ ✗
hints: Divide each data value by the key. All results must be whole numbers.
Try key=5 with all four values.

feedback.correct: Correct! key=5 divides evenly into 15, 10, 20, and 5.
feedback.incorrect: Test each key by dividing all four values. Only key=5 gives all whole numbers.

expected_errors.description: key=2 선택 (Art=10÷2=5는 정수지만 Dance=20÷2=10 심볼 과다)
expected_errors.citation: Van de Walle et al. (2013) p.391

try.math_note: key=5: 3,2,4,1 모두 정수 ✓. 다른 키는 분수 발생.

try.feedback_correct: Correct! key=5 divides evenly into 15, 10, 20, and 5.

try.question: Frequency table: Spring 10, Summer 15, Fall 5, Winter 20. Each symbol = 5 students. What is the TOTAL number of symbols in the picture graph?
try.explanation: Spring=10÷5=2. Summer=15÷5=3. Fall=5÷5=1. Winter=20÷5=4. Total symbols = 2+3+1+4 = 10.
hints: Divide each value by 5 to find symbols per category.
Add all four symbol counts.

feedback.incorrect: First divide each value by 5, then add all symbol counts.

expected_errors.citation: EngageNY G3 M6 — 중간 단계에서 멈춤

try.math_note: 2+3+1+4=10 ✓ 총 심볼 수. 오답 A(50)은 키 변환 전 합계.

practice_r1.question: Frequency table: Swim 14, Run 8, Bike 10. Which key would NOT work well for a picture graph?
practice_r1.explanation: 14 ÷ 3 = 4⅔ (not a clean half). Key = 3 doesn't divide evenly into 14 or 8.
hints: Think about what make picture graphs means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how make picture graphs works.

expected_errors.description: key=3이 Swim(14)에서 깔끔하지 않음을 파악 못하고 key=2(정답 아님) 선택
expected_errors.citation: Van de Walle et al. (2013) p.391

expected_errors.note: key=1 선택 (맞지만 비효율적, 문제는 '잘 맞지 않는' 키)

practice_r1.math_note: 14 ÷ 3 = 4⅔ (not a clean half). Key = 3 doesn't divide evenly into 14 or 8.

practice_r1.question: Each symbol = 6. You need to show 9 students in a picture graph. How many symbols (including half symbols) do you draw?
practice_r1.explanation: 9 ÷ 6 = 1½. Draw 1 full symbol and 1 half symbol.
hints: Divide 9 by the key (6).
If the result is a decimal .5, use a half symbol.

feedback.incorrect: Divide 9 by 6. The answer is 1.5, which means 1 full and 1 half symbol.

expected_errors.citation: Friel et al. (2001) p.130 — 반 심볼 오독

practice_r1.question: Frequency table: Dance 18, Drama 12, Art 6, Music 9. Key = 3. Which category will have the MOST symbols in the picture graph?
practice_r1.explanation: Dance=18÷3=6, Drama=12÷3=4, Art=6÷3=2, Music=9÷3=3. Dance has 6 symbols — the most.
hints: Divide each value by 3 to get the symbol count.
Compare all four symbol counts to find the largest.

feedback.correct: Right! Dance has 6 symbols, more than any other category.
feedback.incorrect: Divide each value by 3 first, then compare symbol counts.

expected_errors.description: 가장 큰 심볼 수(Dance=6) 대신 두 번째(Drama=4) 선택
expected_errors.citation: Van de Walle et al. (2013) p.391

expected_errors.note: 나눗셈 없이 가장 큰 원래 값(18) 카테고리 선택(= 우연히 정답)

practice_r2.question: You collected data: Grapes 18, Melon 12, Berries 24, Peach 6. You want the largest category to have no more than 6 symbols. What is the smallest key you can use?
practice_r2.explanation: Largest = 24. 24 ÷ 6 = 4. If key = 4, symbols: 4½, 3, 6, 1½. Max is 6 symbols. Key = 3 → 24÷3=8 symbols (too many). Key = 4 works.
hints: Think about what make picture graphs means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how make picture graphs works.

expected_errors.citation: Van de Walle et al. (2013) p.391

expected_errors.note: key=6: Peach=6÷6=1, Grapes=18÷6=3, 조건 충족하나 반 심볼 생김

practice_r2.math_note: Largest = 24. 24 ÷ 6 = 4. If key = 4, symbols: 4½, 3, 6, 1½. Max is 6 symbols. Key = 3 → 24÷3=8 symbols (too many). Key = 4 works.

hints: Think about what make picture graphs means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how make picture graphs works.

expected_errors.description: Soup 대신 Salad(6÷2=3) 행 읽어 8-3=5 또는 Pizza-Burger=8-5=3 제출
expected_errors.citation: Van de Walle et al. (2013) p.391

expected_errors.note: 키(÷2) 없이 심볼 수 차이만 계산 (8-4=4이지만 심볼 수만으로 4-2=2)

practice_r2.question: A picture graph shows: Math = 4 symbols, Reading = 3½ symbols, Science = 5 symbols. Each symbol = 4 students. How many students in total does the graph represent?
hints: Multiply each symbol count by 4 (the key).
Add all three results to get the total.

feedback.incorrect: Multiply each symbol count by the key (4), then add all totals.

expected_errors.description: 키 곱하기 없이 심볼 수(4+3½+5=12½)만 합산
expected_errors.citation: Friel et al. (2001) p.130

practice_r2.question: Frequency table: Red 14, Blue 8, Green 10, Purple 4. Key = 2. How many MORE symbols does Red have than Purple?
practice_r2.explanation: Red=14÷2=7 symbols. Purple=4÷2=2 symbols. 7−2=5 more symbols for Red.
hints: Divide each value by 2 to find the symbol count.
Subtract: Red symbols − Purple symbols.

feedback.incorrect: First find each symbol count (÷2), then subtract.

expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.9

expected_errors.note: 나눗셈 없이 원래 값 차이(14-4=10) 제출

hints: Divide each value by 3 to find symbols per category.
Add all four symbol counts.

feedback.incorrect: Divide each value by 3 first, then add all four symbol counts.

practice_r2.question: A tally table shows Soccer: 𝍸𝍸| (2 groups of 5 plus 1 = 11 students). You are making a picture graph where each symbol = 2 students. How many symbols (including half symbols) do you draw for Soccer?
practice_r2.explanation: Soccer tally: 𝍸𝍸| = 5+5+1 = 11. Picture graph: 11÷2 = 5½ symbols.
hints: Count the tally marks first: each 𝍸 = 5, each | = 1.
Then divide the total by the key (2).

feedback.incorrect: First read the tally (11 students), then divide by 2 to find symbols.

expected_errors.citation: Friel et al. (2001) p.130 — 탤리 오독 + 반 심볼 무시

expected_errors.note: 나눗셈 없이 탈리 수(11) 그대로 제출

practice_r2.question: Tally table: Red = 𝍸𝍸𝍸 (15 students), Blue = 𝍸𝍸 (10 students). You make a picture graph with key = 5. How many MORE symbols does Red have than Blue?
practice_r2.explanation: Red=𝍸𝍸𝍸=15. Blue=𝍸𝍸=10. Red symbols=15÷5=3. Blue symbols=10÷5=2. 3−2=1.
hints: Count each tally: 𝍸 = 5. Then divide by key (5).
Subtract: Red symbols − Blue symbols.

feedback.incorrect: Convert tallies to counts (Red=15, Blue=10), then to symbols (÷5), then subtract.

expected_errors.description: 비교 대신 Red 심볼 수(3) 또는 Blue 심볼 수(2)를 답으로 제출
expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.9

expected_errors.note: 원래 탈리 차이(15-10=5) 제출 (심볼 변환 전)

practice_r2.question: Tally data: Monday = 𝍸𝍸 (10), Tuesday = 𝍸𝍸𝍸 (15), Wednesday = 𝍸 (5). Each symbol = 5 in the picture graph. What is the total number of symbols in the picture graph?
hints: Read each tally and convert to a count.
Divide each count by 5, then add.

feedback.incorrect: Read tallies, divide each by 5, then add all symbol counts.

expected_errors.citation: EngageNY G3 M6 — 중간 단계에서 멈춤

practice_r2.math_note: 2+3+1=6 ✓ 오답 C(30)는 키 변환 전 합계. (U1 탈리 읽기 연계)

practice_r3.question: A class of 30 students voted. Results: Soccer 12, Basketball 9, Tennis 6, Volleyball 3. Each symbol = 3. How many total symbols in the graph? Does 12+9+6+3 = 30?
choices: B. 10 symbols, no it doesn't equal 30
D. 8 symbols, no it doesn't equal 30
hints: Think about what make picture graphs means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is A. Remember how make picture graphs works.

expected_errors.description: Soccer(4)만 계산하고 나머지 카테고리 심볼 합산 누락
expected_errors.citation: Friel et al. (2001) p.131

practice_r3.question: Mia's picture graph uses each 🌸 = 4 students. She has 3½ flowers next to 'Park.' Later 6 more students vote for Park. How many flower symbols should be next to Park now?
hints: Think about what make picture graphs means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how make picture graphs works.

expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.10

practice_r3.question: A picture graph shows Drama club = 4 symbols. Each symbol = 6 students. After 12 more students join Drama club, how many symbols will Drama have?
practice_r3.explanation: Drama original: 4×6=24 students. Add 12: 24+12=36. New symbols: 36÷6=6.
hints: First find the original number of students (symbols × key).
Add 12, then divide by the key again.

feedback.incorrect: Step 1: 4×6=24 students. Step 2: 24+12=36. Step 3: 36÷6=6.

expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.10

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 2 Lesson 2.3 — Make Picture Graphs

assessment_source.name: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Sample Items — 3.MD.B.3
assessment_source.description: 3.MD.B.3 평가 지침 — 그림 그래프 만들기 및 키 선택 문제

practice_r3.question: Frequency table: Spring 12, Summer 18, Fall 6, Winter 9. Which key results in ALL categories using only WHOLE number symbols (no half symbols)?
practice_r3.explanation: key=3: Spring=12÷3=4 ✓, Summer=18÷3=6 ✓, Fall=6÷3=2 ✓, Winter=9÷3=3 ✓ — all whole. key=2: Winter=9÷2=4½ ✗. key=4: Summer=18÷4=4.5 ✗. key=6: Winter=9÷6=1.5 ✗.
solution_steps: key=2: Winter=9÷2=4½ ✗. key=4: Summer=18÷4=4.5 ✗. key=6: Winter=9÷6=1.5 ✗
hints: Divide every value (12, 18, 6, 9) by each key. All four results must be whole numbers.
Try key=3 first.

feedback.correct: Correct! key=3 divides evenly into 12, 18, 6, and 9.
feedback.incorrect: Test each key with all four values. key=3 gives 4, 6, 2, 3 — all whole numbers.

expected_errors.description: key=2 선택: Winter=9÷2=4½ (반 심볼 필요 → 정수 조건 위반)
expected_errors.citation: Van de Walle et al. (2013) p.391

expected_errors.note: key=6: Winter=9÷6=1½ (반 심볼 필요 → 정수 조건 위반)

practice_r3.math_note: key=3: 4,6,2,3 모두 정수 ✓. 다른 키는 Winter 또는 Summer에서 소수 발생.
